denial_letter_references.title: ProPublica — Uncovered (insurance denials reporting)
denial_letter_references.relevance: Calibrated P2 letter surface: Circular medical-necessity boilerplate.

denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

UnitedHealthcare has completed its medical necessity review of the request submitted by your provider and received at our office on August 3, 2026. This letter constitutes our coverage determination dated August 5, 2026, and also addresses the appeal associated with this request.

After review under the Optum behavioral health Level of Care Guidelines, we are unable to approve the requested service. The clinical criteria for the requested service are not met under the cited guideline module. The criteria applied are characterized in the ages 12 to 17 population and call for documentation consistent with that population; on that basis the request does not satisfy the threshold requirements in the referenced criteria set.

Our records indicate that the appeal previously submitted in connection with this request was closed as withdrawn at the member's request. No further action is pending on that appeal.

If you disagree with this decision, you may file an internal appeal within the time permitted under your plan by contacting the number on your member ID card. You may also request an external review by an independent review organization.

We understand this decision may be disappointing. This determination addresses coverage under your plan only and is not medical advice.

Sincerely,
UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
clinical_context: The patient is a 22-year-old man with combined-type attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (F90.2), diagnosed in adulthood after a structured evaluation that documented inattentive and hyperactive-impulsive symptoms with clear occupational impairment, including repeatedly missed deadlines and a formal performance warning at work. He completed a 10-week trial of atomoxetine 80 mg daily with only a partial response, and his psychiatrist requested continued stimulant medication management with structured adult ADHD coaching. Adult ADHD is a recognized DSM-5 diagnosis, and authorization for an adult's treatment is governed by adult ADHD standards; pediatric utilization criteria written for the ages 12 to 17 population do not apply to a 22-year-old. The member did not withdraw his appeal: neither he nor his psychiatrist submitted any withdrawal request, yet the plan recorded the appeal as withdrawn and stopped the appeal clock. The submitted records document the adult evaluation, the atomoxetine trial, and the functional impairment at work.

matrix_coverage.reasoning: matrix_cell matches the assigned cell field-for-field; F90.2 is behavioral_health; adult ADHD management is realistic; rationale instantiates guideline_mis_cite.

scenario_realism.reasoning: UHC/Optum applying pediatric ADHD utilization criteria to an adult and wrongly closing an appeal as withdrawn are authentic exploitable defects; failed atomoxetine and occupational impairment rebut cleanly.

insurer_voice.reasoning: UnitedHealthcare branding, Optum behavioral-health Level of Care Guidelines, UM sign-off; no cross-insurer tells.

denial_logic.reasoning: Circular guideline_mis_cite logic citing an unnamed Optum module with no specified threshold, applying ages-12-17 criteria; authentically shoddy, fits guideline_mis_cite.

clinical_realism.reasoning: Adult-diagnosed combined ADHD, atomoxetine 80mg x10 weeks partial response, occupational impairment with performance warning, stimulant + coaching request; realistic pathway.

diagnosis_treatment_match.reasoning: Stimulant management plus ADHD coaching is standard multimodal care for ADHD; clean match.

diversity_delta.reasoning: No neighbour shares ADHD or the stimulant-maintenance-plus-coaching trajectory; clinical content distinct from the BED stimulant cases.

diversity_statistical.reasoning: Max trigram-Jaccard: corpus=0.126 (nearest=case_114_aetna_mednec), neighbours=0.0. Thresholds: warn>=0.35, fail>=0.5.

safety_redactor.reasoning: No banned topics; ADHD stimulant treatment without SI is allowed; adult 22M (no gender-dysphoria framing); in-scope commercial plan.

contradiction_hunter.reasoning: Gender, diagnosis, treatment, dates stable; the ages-12-17 criteria and the closed-as-withdrawn claim are appealable falsehoods the record rebuts, not unstable scenario facts.

llm_tell_detector.reasoning: Formal insurer boilerplate and dense human-style chart prose; no GPT vocabulary, listicles, or meta-commentary.

overall_tone.reasoning: Cold procedural UHC register with realistic obfuscation; no apology; flat factual delivery of the withdrawn-appeal claim; neutral clinical narrative.

financial_auditor.reasoning: Silent on cost liability (standard practice); no dollar figures; no Medicare/Medicaid conflation.

legal_auditor.reasoning: No invented statutes, no Medicare framing; appeal window left vague and ERISA disclosures omitted; the closed-as-withdrawn clock-stop is a realistic procedural irregularity.

demographic_validator.reasoning: 22M with adult-diagnosed combined ADHD is epidemiologically sound; consistent male pronouns; the ages-12-17 line is a payer-criteria defect, not a profile mismatch.

scope_guard.reasoning: UHC in-scope, self-funded employer-sponsored commercial, adult 22, no live-filing/representation language.

date_sanity.reasoning: Received Aug 3 / determined Aug 5 2026 in correct order; appeal handling temporally coherent; 10-week atomoxetine trial plausible.

citation_traceability.reasoning: Real Optum behavioral-health Level of Care Guidelines named at umbrella level with no module code/edition; realistically vague.

appeal_difficulty.reasoning: Pediatric (ages 12-17) criteria applied to a 22-year-old adult, an appeal falsely closed as withdrawn stopping the clock, and ignored adult evaluation/atomoxetine failure. Easy multi-vector appeal.
exploitable_weaknesses: Wrong-population guideline: pediatric (ages 12-17) Level of Care criteria applied to a 22-year-old adult with DSM-5 adult ADHD, rendering the cited criteria set facially inapplicable
Procedural violation: appeal falsely recorded as withdrawn at the member's request and the appeal clock stopped, though neither the member nor his psychiatrist submitted any withdrawal

Vague/circular reasoning: cites unmet 'threshold requirements in the referenced criteria set' without identifying any specific applicable adult standard, and ignores the documented adult evaluation, failed atomoxetine trial, and occupational impairment
strong_defenses: The plan can point to a facially complete review trail — a dated coverage determination citing a named guideline framework (Optum Behavioral Health Level of Care Guidelines) and preserved external-review rights — arguing a structured medical-necessity process was followed even if the specific criteria module was misapplied
